cooling_type: air-cooled (LPU is air-cooled by design), liquid cooling capability available

facility_name: Oregon State University CEOAS Data Center (ZutaCore Retrofit)
opportunity_summary: University research data center retrofitted with ZutaCore HyperCool waterless two-phase direct-to-chip cooling. 30% lower processor temps, 20% higher compute performance. Eliminated 4 air cooling systems. Heat recaptured for campus building heating. Heterogeneous fleet (Dell, HP, Supermicro with NVIDIA/AMD). University HPC centers are typically under 5MW, making this a strong small-facility indicator.
gpu_model: NVIDIA and AMD processors (models not specified)

facility_name: Dataprana AI Data Center — Galveston County
cooling_type: immersion cooling (Dataprana specializes in immersion-cooled facilities)
opportunity_summary: Ultra-small 1.5MW AI data center in Galveston County, TX. Joint venture with DC Futureminds. Dataprana specializes in immersion-cooled facilities (flagship 30MW immersion site in La Marque, TX). This is a proof-of-concept site targeting GPUaaS providers. Extremely small facility — ideal for small-scale cooling vendor engagement.

facility_name: UT Arlington ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS Data Center Testbed
cooling_type: hybrid (Accelsius MR250 two-phase CDU + rear door heat exchangers)
opportunity_summary: DOE-funded data center cooling testbed at UT Arlington, led by Prof. Dereje Agonafer (National Academy of Engineering). Accelsius MR250 250kW two-phase CDU deployed. Target: reduce cooling energy from 40% to under 5% of total IT load. Proof of concept by H1 2026. Partners include JetCool, Intel, Raytheon, HP, NVIDIA, Flexnode. University-scale facility likely under 5MW.

PUE 1.1 + liquid cooling at 20MW. Colovore's third facility, purpose-built for high-density AI/HPC with up to 250kW per rack via DTC and rear-door cooling. Groundbreaking Feb 2025. Larger than 10MW threshold but PUE and cooling match strongly. Colovore is a liquid-cooling-native operator — competitor landscape intelligence value.

PUE ~1.1 + liquid cooling at 20MW. Fourth Colovore location, pre-leasing now for 2027 launch. Larger than 10MW but matches on PUE and cooling. Pre-construction phase means cooling vendor decisions still open.

facility_name: Sabey SDC Austin — TACC Horizon Supercomputer
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (DLC) + air hybrid, 140kW/cabinet GPU density
opportunity_summary: Sabey's first Texas campus at 85MW total, with 15-20MW allocated to TACC Horizon (largest academic supercomputer in NSF portfolio). Dell-built system using 100% direct liquid cooling with 140kW/cabinet GPU density and immersion cooling. 6x more energy-efficient than air-cooled. 300 petaflops peak FP64. Spring 2026 launch. Campus total is large but TACC deployment is in 15-20MW range.

facility_name: Equinix DC15 CIF — Accelsius NeuCool Deployment
opportunity_summary: Equinix's Co-Innovation Facility at DC15 Ashburn with Accelsius NeuCool IR80 two-phase DTC system deployed Q3 2025. Partnership catalyzed by shared ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS participation. NeuCool allows 6-8C warmer inlet water = more free cooling. CIF is a showcase/testbed but signals Equinix's cooling vendor evaluation pipeline. Competitive intelligence value for cooling vendors.

Colovore's 'Project Raptor' — $500M investment on 30-acre Ironwood Tract near Austin. Part of Colovore's expansion from 10MW to 50MW total. PUE 1.1 (company standard). Liquid cooling + closed-loop water. Construction early 2026. Pre-construction = ideal timing for cooling vendor engagement.

cooling_type: hybrid immersion + cold plate liquid cooling
opportunity_summary: Ultra-small 1MW edge data center in Long Island City, NYC. ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS-funded ($3.5M) technology: manifold microchannel heatsink + hybrid immersion cooling + additive-manufactured dry cooler. 80kW capable racks with precision immersion and cold plate. GPU and CPU solutions. Digital twin running. Company plans 'a couple more' US sites. Ideal small-facility target.

PUE 1.2 + liquid cooling at 15.2MW. Metrobloks is a 2024-founded developer specializing in high-density liquid-cooled modular colocation in metro areas. Miami facility features up to 150kW/rack, hybrid air + DTC liquid cooling, 22MW reserved utility power. Q4 2026 launch. Ultra-low latency to NAP of the Americas. Pre-construction = cooling vendor decisions open.

facility_name: ORNL Summit Supercomputer — Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (cold plates on CPUs/GPUs) + rear-door heat exchangers
opportunity_summary: Summit consumes ~15MW with warm-water (70F) DLC cold plates on all CPUs and GPUs plus rear-door heat exchangers. Cooling drops to 10% of ops cost vs predecessors. Being decommissioned as Frontier/Discovery come online — cooling lessons inform next-gen DOE systems.

cooling_type: liquid cooling (water-cooled, 44,000 gal system with cooling towers/chillers/heat exchangers)
opportunity_summary: DOE exascale system consuming ~39MW (60MW facility allocation). Liquid-cooled with 44,000-gallon water system through pipes 4-30 inches diameter. 14 substations provide 60MW for Aurora + building + future systems (Solstice/Equinox). 60,000+ Intel GPUs.

facility_name: TACC Stampede3 Supercomputer — J.J. Pickle Research Campus
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (Dell PowerEdge XE9640 DLC servers)
opportunity_summary: NSF flagship supercomputer with Dell PowerEdge XE9640 DLC servers using Intel Data Center GPU Max accelerators. TACC facility total capacity 9MW max (6MW typical). Stampede3 is 10 PFLOPS. DLC enables AI/ML acceleration on the GPU partition. Serving open science 2024-2029.

facility_name: NERSC Perlmutter Supercomputer — Shyh Wang Hall, LBNL
operator_company: National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC)
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (DLC cold plates on GPUs/CPUs, free cooling)
opportunity_summary: PUE 1.05-1.08, among world's best. DLC cold plates on 1,536 GPU nodes (NVIDIA A100) and 3,072 CPU nodes. Free cooling via water towers (no mechanical chillers) in Berkeley climate. Up to 250kW per rack. TDP 6.9MW nameplate. Exceptional efficiency reference site.

facility_name: NCSA DeltaAI Supercomputer — National Petascale Computing Facility
cooling_type: 100% water cooled (HPE Cray EX2500 cabinets, zero fans)
opportunity_summary: 100% water-cooled with zero fans. 4 HPE Cray EX2500 cabinets at 135kW each (~540kW total). NVIDIA H100 and GH200 Grace Hopper Superchips. 633 PFLOPS FP16 for AI/ML. Compact 3-rack compute footprint demonstrates extreme DLC density. NSF's most performant GPU resource.

facility_name: LANL Mission & Vision Supercomputers — Los Alamos
cooling_type: direct liquid cooling (HPE Cray GX5000, NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture)
opportunity_summary: Two next-gen supercomputers: Mission (4x faster than Crossroads) and Vision. Direct liquid-cooled HPE Cray GX5000 with NVIDIA Vera Rubin GPUs and Quantum-X800 InfiniBand. Part of DOE $370M investment. Mission supports nuclear stockpile; Vision for AI research. Expected 2027.

facility_name: Sandia HPC Center — Submer Immersion Cooling Pilot
cooling_type: direct immersion cooling (Submer Technologies, non-conductive fluid)
opportunity_summary: Immersion cooling testbed. Full servers submerged in non-conductive fluid (Submer Technologies, Barcelona). Captures 100% of heat, may reduce power by up to 70%. Zero water evaporation. Waste heat usable for building heating. Case study expected. Complements earlier Astra warm-water cooling (1.2MW, HPE MCS-300 thermosyphon saving 2.5M gal/yr).

facility_name: IREN Childress Horizon 1 — 75MW Liquid-Cooled AI DC

opportunity_summary: 75MW liquid-cooled AI data center (Horizon 1) at Childress, TX. Direct-to-chip cooling at 200kW/rack for NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. $300-350M investment. Part of 750MW site (450MW energized). Transitioning from Bitcoin mining to AI/HPC. Additional 2GW planned at Sweetwater sites.

opportunity_summary: DOES NOT CURRENTLY QUALIFY. 137-acre campus, 100MW expansion H2 2026. Clients use advanced cooling for AI/GPU. $240M acquisition + $1.99B securitization. Strong DLC potential.

facility_name: Meta Beaver Dam AI Campus — Dodge County, WI
opportunity_summary: Meta's 30th DC worldwide, 26th in US. 700,000 sq ft on 500-acre site. Dry-cooling eliminates water demand. Meta has separately announced company-wide shift to direct-to-chip liquid cooling for AI workloads. Mortenson as GC.
notes: Meta has announced company-wide shift to cold-plate DTC liquid cooling for AI. Beaver Dam campus announced as dry-cooled; may include DTC for AI racks within dry-cooled hall design. Commissioning 2027.

facility_name: Modine Airedale Manufacturing Facility — Franklin, WI
opportunity_summary: Cooling vendor manufacturing hub. Modine makes Airedale data center cooling solutions. 155,000 sq ft facility, 300+ jobs. Part of $100M multi-year investment. Competitor intelligence — Modine is a cooling vendor, not a customer.
notes: This is a COOLING VENDOR manufacturing facility, not a data center. Included for competitive intelligence. Modine founded in Racine in 1916. Also expanding in Grand Prairie TX, Grenada MS, Jefferson City MO.

opportunity_summary: Stargate-branded 1GW+ AI data center campus. Closed-loop non-evaporative liquid cooling (Oracle standard). Three 550K sq ft buildings on 250 acres. Part of OpenAI's $500B Stargate initiative. Walbridge as GC. DTE Energy supplying power with battery storage.
notes: Dubbed 'The Barn' for the historic red barn preserved at the entrance. Pending Michigan Public Service Commission approval. DTE Energy providing power with battery storage entirely financed by the project. 2,500 union construction jobs, 450+ permanent jobs.

opportunity_summary: xAI's $20B expansion into Mississippi. Adjoins Memphis Colossus 1+2 complex. DLC for extreme GPU density. 2GW total cluster capacity. Graywater treatment plant ($80M) to reuse 13M liters/day. Expected Blackwell GB200 and next-gen GPU deployment.
notes: Adjacent to xAI's Colossus 1+2 in Memphis, TN. xAI built $80M graywater treatment plant to reuse ~13M liters/day. Colossus 1 has 230,000+ GPUs including 30,000+ GB200 Blackwell. Colossus 2 bringing 550,000 Blackwell-gen chips online. MACROHARDRR target: 1M GPUs by late 2026.

facility_name: Meta Hyperion AI Campus — Richland Parish, LA
opportunity_summary: World's largest planned AI data center. 5GW total planned, 1.5GW+ Phase 1. $27B joint venture with Blue Owl Capital. 4M sq ft core build. Meta roadmap includes DTC liquid cooling for AI racks. Tent-like prefab construction inspired by xAI. 10 gas-fired power plants ordered.
notes: Meta plans shift to DTC liquid cooling company-wide. Hyperion uses tent-like prefab structures with modular power+cooling. 10 gas-fired power plants ordered (Mar 2026), tripling initial plans. Three combined-cycle turbines (2,260 MW) plus ~100 miles new transmission. Largest single AI DC by end 2027.

facility_name: Vultr Springfield AI Supercluster — Clark County, OH (AMD MI355X)
opportunity_summary: 24,000 AMD MI355X GPUs (1,400W liquid-primary). MI355X spec requires liquid cooling. $901M-$1.3B investment. Former LexisNexis facility. Expected to integrate AMD MI450 and Helios rack-scale infrastructure next.
notes: AMD MI355X has 1,400W TDP and is liquid-primary by design. Facility described as 'low-water' but liquid cooling implied by GPU choice. 300,000 gallons/day water use estimate. Also slated for AMD MI450 and Helios rack-scale next-gen.

facility_name: Joule BetterGrid Campus — Millard County, UT (4GW Self-Powered)
opportunity_summary: Self-powered data center campus on 4,000 acres. Closed-loop DTC liquid cooling saves millions of gallons vs evaporative. Caterpillar G3520K generators + 1.1GWh battery storage. 455MW Q4 2026, scaling to 4GW. Over 4,800 ft elevation for natural cooling efficiency. Combined cooling, heat and power system.
notes: Unique self-powered model using Caterpillar generators + battery. Not grid-dependent. Combined cooling, heat and power (CCHP) system. Altitude provides natural cooling advantage. Could eventually scale to 12GW.

facility_name: Novva Data Centers SLC Campus — West Jordan, UT (DTC AI Expansion)
opportunity_summary: One of the largest DTC-cooled AI data centers in the world upon completion. Waterless cooling system. Phase 3: 318,000 sq ft, 72MW critical IT. Total campus: 1M sq ft, 175MW when complete. $2B CIM Group financing. Also operates in Colorado Springs and Las Vegas.
notes: Novva promotes waterless cooling as competitive differentiator. Also operates Colorado Springs and Las Vegas locations. CIM Group $2B financing commitment for western US expansion.

facility_name: Novva Colorado Springs Campus — CO (Waterless Cooling Expansion)
cooling_type: ambient air + waterless cooling (8,000+ hrs/yr free cooling)
opportunity_summary: 68-acre campus near Denver. 8,000+ hours ambient mountain air cooling. Currently 6MW, expanding to 30-40MW with $200M investment. Plans for 100% renewable energy and waterless cooling. 250,000 sq ft expansion planned.
notes: Currently 6MW, smaller facility suitable for cooling vendor partnership at expansion phase. Mountain climate provides natural cooling advantage. Part of Novva's CIM Group $2B western US expansion.

facility_name: Crusoe Spark Factory — Brighton, CO (Liquid-Cooled Modular AI)
opportunity_summary: Crusoe manufacturing facility for liquid-cooled modular AI factories (Crusoe Spark). 352,000 sq ft, $200M+. Liquid-cooled version launching H2 2026. First factory-produced modules Q3 2026. This is a manufacturing site for prefab liquid-cooled DC modules.
notes: This is a MANUFACTURING facility for modular liquid-cooled AI DCs, not a DC itself. Crusoe's energy background (flared gas utilization) positions them uniquely. Competitive intelligence — Crusoe is both a cooling integrator and compute provider.

facility_name: Stargate Project Jupiter — Santa Teresa, NM (Dona Ana County)
opportunity_summary: $165B Stargate campus. 4 hyperscale buildings on 1,400 acres. Closed-loop DTC liquid cooling — one-time fill of ~10M gallons, then 20K gallons/day average. 700-900MW dedicated microgrid (gas turbines exceeding EPA by 50%). NVIDIA GB200 Blackwell racks. Operational end of 2026.
notes: Part of $500B Stargate initiative. NM site taps western renewables and long-haul transmission. Gas turbines exceed EPA standards by 50%. State incentive environment favorable. Near El Paso, TX border.

facility_name: Edged Des Moines — Ankeny, IA (Waterless AI Data Center)
cooling_type: closed-loop waterless + plug-and-play liquid cooling (up to 200kW/rack)
opportunity_summary: 13MW AI-optimized modular DC. Closed-loop waterless cooling. Supports up to 70kW/rack air and 200kW/rack with plug-and-play liquid cooling integration. Groundbreaking May 2025. Completion 2027.
notes: Edged specializes in modular, waterless, AI-optimized DCs. 13MW facility with 200kW/rack liquid cooling is a strong match for cooling vendors. Construction started H2 2025, completion 2027.

facility_name: Microsoft West Des Moines 6th Campus (Project Ruthenium) — IA
opportunity_summary: Microsoft's 6th Iowa campus. Zero-water cooling technology (closed-loop DTC). Part of $5-6B total Iowa investment. Construction start 2026. Also Ginger West campus getting zero-water tech.
notes: Microsoft's 6th campus in the Des Moines metro. Zero-water tech = closed-loop DTC liquid cooling (Microsoft standard for all new AI DCs). Also applying zero-water to Ginger West campus buildings.

facility_name: Meta Kuna Data Center — Kuna, ID
opportunity_summary: 960,000 sq ft Meta DC. $800M investment. 200MW renewable energy. Closed-loop water-efficient cooling. Water positive by 2030. Operational end of 2026 (delayed from 2025). 1,400 construction workers at peak.
notes: Meta water positive by 2030 commitment. Idaho legislature advancing data center water consumption bill (2026). Completion delayed from 2025 to end 2026.

facility_name: ValorC3 Boise — Emerald Street, ID (AI-Ready Colocation)
cooling_type: air and liquid cooling with closed-loop system
opportunity_summary: 10MW AI-ready colocation in Boise. Air and liquid cooling with closed-loop for minimal water waste. Expanding to 12MW before Phase 1 complete. Near Boise City Hall West. Strong small-facility cooling vendor opportunity.
notes: 10MW facility is excellent small-facility target for cooling vendors. Closed-loop liquid cooling. Expanding before even opening Phase 1. Operational expected 2027.

facility_name: Big Sky Digital Infrastructure — Billings, MT (Quantica/EnCap 1GW Campus)
cooling_type: evaluating zero-water air, deep aquifer, greywater, DTC liquid
opportunity_summary: 5,000-acre energy and digital campus outside Billings. 500MW initial, 1GW by 2030. Evaluating multiple cooling approaches including DTC liquid. Renewable + battery energy storage. NorthWestern Energy providing power. EnCap-backed. Cooling decision not yet finalized = opportunity window for vendors.
notes: Cooling technology decision pending — this is a PRE-DECISION sales window for cooling vendors. Quantica currently has no water rights in Montana per state DNRC. Montana PSC reviewing DC regulatory framework. NorthWestern Energy tapped for power. Construction could begin 2026, first operations 2028.

facility_name: Related Digital Cheyenne Campus — WY (302MW, CoreWeave Anchor)
opportunity_summary: $1.2B campus. CoreWeave 88MW anchor tenant. Air-cooled chillers, nominal water. 302MW total across 115 acres. Clayco as builder. Phase 1 late 2026. Wyoming's climate supports air cooling efficiency but CoreWeave's GPU workloads may eventually require liquid cooling upgrade.
notes: Currently air-cooled but CoreWeave's AI GPU workloads (Blackwell) will likely require liquid cooling for future phases. Potential retrofit opportunity. Wyoming hosting closed-door data center summit (Mar 2026) to attract more developers.

facility_name: Google Lincoln (Agate) — Lancaster County, NE (2M sq ft)
opportunity_summary: Google's 3rd Nebraska data center. 2M sq ft on 570+ acres. Could require 1-3GW power (more than all of Lincoln). $1.2B+ investment. Cooling design not yet public — Google has deployed 1GW of liquid cooling globally for TPU pods. Pre-decision opportunity for cooling vendors.
notes: Cooling design not yet public for this site. Google has 1GW liquid cooling deployed globally for TPU pods and contributing cooling tech to OCP (Project Deschutes CDU). Scale suggests liquid cooling will be required for AI workloads. Originally filed as Agate LLC in 2019.

facility_name: Alcove Osawatomie Data Center — Miami County, KS (Predevelopment)
opportunity_summary: Early-stage predevelopment. 115 acres, 600K sq ft, 50-150MW, $900M-$1.1B. No construction approved, no land sold. 3-year exclusive development agreement. 1M gallons water/day + 150MW power required. Kansas SB 98 tax exemption (20yr) in effect. Very early opportunity window.
notes: Very early stage — no construction, no land sale, no tenant. Kansas SB 98 (July 2025) provides 20-year tax exemption. Miami County Commission considered construction moratorium. Cooling tech undecided = opportunity window but high risk of project not proceeding.
